title: Fatty Acids and Derivatives Group - Canada.ca
content:
## Fatty Acids and Derivatives Group
- Fatty Acids and Derivatives Group
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). The screening assessment focuses on 9 of 16 substances referred to collectively as the Fatty Acids and Derivatives Group.
- Summary of publications
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Fatty Acids and Derivatives Group
None planned at this time
α-Linolenic acid (ALA)
9,12,15-Octadecatrienoic acid, (Z,Z,Z)
Fatty acids, tall-oil
Tall oil fatty acids, potassium salts
Fatty acids, tall-oil, potassium salts
Evening Primrose Oil (EPO)
Evening primrose, Oenothera biennis, ext.
Fatty acids, C18-unsaturated, trimers
Fatty acids, C18-unsaturated, dimers
Fatty acids, coco, 2-ethylhexyl esters
Publication of the final screening assessment. The  related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 154, No. 31 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 33 .
Seven other substances were identified as being a part of the Fatty Acids and Derivatives Group at the outset of the third phase of the CMP .
One of the 7 substances (CAS RN 8002-65-1) was originally  included in the Draft Screening Assessment  for the Fatty Acids and Derivatives Group published on August 18, 2018;  however, based on additional information received, this UVCB (Unknown or  Variable Composition, Complex Reaction Products or Biological Materials)  substance requires further assessment. Therefore, further evaluation of this substance  will be provided in a separate screening assessment.
Four of the 7 substances [CAS RNs 68139-89-9,  53980-88-4, 68647-55-2, and Domestic Substances List (DSL) Confidential  Accession Number 11556-0] were subsequently determined to be of low concern to  both human health and the environment, through other approaches.   Conclusions for CAS RN 68139-89-9 are provided in the Final Screening Assessment for Substances Identified as Being  of Low Concern using on the Ecological Risk Classification of Organic  Substances and the Threshold of Toxicological Concern (TTC)-based Approach for  Certain Substances . Conclusions for CAS RNs 53980-88-4,  68647-55-2, and DSL Confidential Accession Number 11556-0 are provided in  the Final Screening Assessment for the Rapid Screening of  Substances with Limited General Population Exposure .
The remaining 2 substances (CAS RNs 68476-03-9 and  73138-45-1) were placed into another substance group to which they are more  appropriately suited on the basis of scientific  considerations. Conclusions for these substances are provided in the Final Screening Assessment for Seven Hydrocarbon-based  Substances .
